title: Military Oath and Constitutional Framing
summary: Drop #11 emphasized military loyalty to the Constitution and suggested that patriotic duty superseded partisan politics.
raw_text:
Why do we serve?
Why do we protect the Constitution?
What is the military oath?
Why is this relevant?
Q

lines.fact: Members of the U.S. military swear an oath to support and defend the Constitution of the United States.
lines.theory: Q followers interpreted this as evidence that military actors were conducting constitutional defense operations behind the scenes.
lines.context: Military symbolism and constitutional language were recurring themes in early Q narratives.
lines.questions: What evidence would demonstrate active military intervention in civilian governance?
